meaning: escape; or leave for a holiday
example:
We need to get away for a weekend.
The thief got away.
I couldn't get away from work.

When did you get back?
I'll get back home by six.
Get back to me soon.

Go ahead, I'm listening.
They went ahead with the plan.
Can I go ahead and book it?

meaning: explode or ring; or (of food) become bad
example:
The alarm went off at six.
The milk has gone off.
The fireworks went off.

Don't hang up!
She hung up on me.
He hung up the phone.

We hang out at weekends.
They hung out at the cafe.
Want to hang out later?

Hurry up, we're late!
Tell him to hurry up.
Hurry up and finish.

Log in with your email.
I can't log in.
She logged in to check.

meaning: feel happy about a future event
example:
I look forward to seeing you.
We look forward to the trip.
She's looking forward to it.

Put away your toys.
I put the dishes away.
Put your phone away.

Put down that knife.
She put the bag down.
Put it down gently.

The dog ran away.
Don't run away from problems.
They ran away together.

Sign up for the class.
I signed up online.
Hundreds signed up.

Slow down, you're too fast.
The car slowed down.
You need to slow down and rest.

I stayed up late.
Don't stay up too long.
We stayed up talking.

Take down the poster.
She took down my number.
They took down the tent.

I'm working on my English.
She's working on a project.
We need to work on this.

Calm down, it's okay.
He calmed down quickly.
Take a breath and calm down.

Get in the car.
What time does the train get in?
We got in late.

I want to go back home.
Let's go back.
She went back to work.
